options.label: Adopt now, defer test
options.summary: Make Sonnet-plans/Haiku-implements the default Do-phase pattern immediately, without waiting for the pre-registered 4-arm benchmark to run.

options.label: Run pre-registered benchmark, decide by result
options.summary: Execute the registered 4-arm test (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus-solo + Tiered) and adopt only if it meets the pre-set quality/cost decision rule.

options.label: Reject tiering, keep single-model
options.summary: Keep Sonnet-solo (or configurable single top-tier model) as the Do-phase executor; do not pursue tiered orchestration further.

options.label: Defer decision, no spend yet
options.summary: Postpone both adoption and the benchmark run until the three unresolved owner calls (grader independence, hermetic controls, retry/feedback format) are settled.

options.label: Full pre-registered grid
options.summary: Run all 4 arms (Haiku-solo, Sonnet-solo, Opus-solo, Tiered) across all 8 pre-screened tasks at 3 reps each before any analysis.

options.label: Pilot Tiered arm first
options.summary: Run Tiered vs Sonnet-solo baseline first at pre-registered reps; check the delta/token decision rule; stop before scaling other arms if already decidable.

options.label: Pilot Opus-solo first
options.summary: Run the costliest-per-invocation arm, Opus-solo, plus Sonnet-solo baseline first to test reachability of the 'Opus-quality at sub-Opus-cost' prize before full-grid spend.

options.label: Pre-registered sequential escalation
options.summary: Run all 4 arms at minimal reps first, adding reps/tasks only where a CI straddles the decision boundary, per Comment 1's stated escalation method.

options.label: Prose sync-before-spend rule
options.summary: Add a CLAUDE.md rule requiring git fetch + re-read of the issue/PRs before executing and again before final push.

options.label: Claim protocol via issue comment/assignee
options.summary: Sessions post an 'in progress' comment or self-assign on the issue when starting work, clearing it on completion.

options.label: Both sync rule and claim protocol
options.summary: Combine the prose sync-before-spend rule with the claim protocol to cover both unclaimed and long-running collisions.

options.label: No new mechanism
options.summary: Leave coordination as-is, treating the duplicate-spend incident as an isolated, tolerable cost of the current workflow.

options.label: Run full routing experiment now
options.summary: Execute the complete grid across checker variants immediately, reusing the benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution harness as described.

options.label: Gate on checker fidelity first
options.summary: Test checker false-accept rate first; only proceed to the full grid if a checker variant beats ~10-15% false-accept.

options.label: Decline the experiment
options.summary: Do not run the routing experiment; keep Sonnet-solo as the default given the pre-registered 25% false-accept threat.

options.label: Defer with no urgency
options.summary: Postpone the decision to a later time, per the issue's own timing note that it is the owner's call with no urgency.

options.label: Cap at 1,000/day
options.summary: Set the free-tier cap at 1,000 requests/day, matching the already-built and load-tested metering capacity.

options.label: Cap near observed usage (~150/day)
options.summary: Set the cap close to typical active-key usage (~100-250/day) so it functions as a conversion lever toward Pro.

options.label: Intermediate cap with planned revisit
options.summary: Choose a cap between observed usage and built capacity, with a defined point to reassess once Pro conversion data exists.

options.label: Narrow extraction (dispatcher console)
options.summary: Extract dispatcher console as a single service behind a thin API, tables remain in shared database for now.

options.label: Broad multi-service migration
options.summary: Commit to platform-wide migration, sequencing multiple service extractions over the next two quarters.

options.label: Status quo
options.summary: Keep Ops Tools on the shared deploy train; no extraction undertaken at this time.

options.label: Keep cron script
options.summary: Continue running nightly_sync.py via cron as-is; revisit adoption if job count or failure rate crosses a stated threshold.

options.label: Adopt Conductor now
options.summary: Stand up the Conductor orchestration framework (scheduler, metadata DB, workers) and migrate the single sync job onto it.

options.label: Lightweight retry-and-alert wrapper
options.summary: Add a small wrapper providing retries and alerting around the existing script, without adopting a full framework.

options.label: Staged sunset with floor and migration help
options.summary: Set a firm sunset date (e.g. six months out) with a usage floor, staged notices, and hands-on migration assistance for the three loud accounts.

options.label: Indefinite retention
options.summary: Keep v1 running indefinitely, continuing to maintain both code paths until complaints about sunsetting stop.

options.label: Aggressive 60-day sunset
options.summary: Retire v1 within 60 days based solely on the 0.4% traffic share, without further per-customer cost investigation.
